pi_items.source: Official OrientDB documentation - demodb is built-in since v3.0 (https://orientdb.dev/docs/3.2.x/gettingstarted/demodb/index.html); confirmed in seed_demodb.py, setup_orientdb.sh, and task scripts
verifier.py:80-84 checks for hotels_name.get('mandatory') is True; Hotels class exists in demodb schema per OrientDB documentation
verifier.py:87-91 checks for hotels_name.get('notNull') is True; NOTNULL is valid OrientDB property constraint
verifier.py:98 expects string '1'; metadata specifies min: '1' as string
verifier.py:106 expects string '5'; metadata specifies max: '5' as string
verifier.py:116-120 checks for prof_email.get('mandatory') is True; Profiles class exists in demodb per OrientDB docs and seed_demodb.py
verifier.py:122-126 checks for prof_email.get('notNull') is True; Email property is created in seed_demodb.py:106
verifier.py:134-141 converts to float and compares float(min_price) == 0; metadata specifies min: '0' as string which converts to 0.0
verifier.py:147-151 checks for rest_name.get('mandatory') is True; Restaurants class exists in demodb per OrientDB documentation
verifier.py:153-157 checks for rest_name.get('notNull') is True; Restaurants.Name property created in seed_demodb.py:133
verifier.py:161 checks for reviews.get('strictMode') is True; STRICTMODE is valid OrientDB ALTER CLASS feature per documentation (https://orientdb.com/docs/last/sql/SQL-Alter-Class.html)
setup_orientdb.sh:6-7 sets JAVA_HOME and PATH for OrientDB; install_orientdb.sh downloads and installs OrientDB; demodb is built-in feature of OrientDB v3.0+

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic travel agency database - generated by seed_demodb.py at runtime
pi_items.source: seed_demodb.py comment:3-5 states 'Seed OrientDB DemoDB with real travel agency data. Schema matches OrientDB's official DemoDB'; data is created via INSERT statements, not downloaded

pi_items.verified_value: All classes exist: Vertex classes Countries, Profiles, Customers, Hotels, Restaurants, Attractions (with subclasses), Orders, Reviews; Edge classes HasFriend, HasProfile, HasCustomer, HasOrder, HasStayed, HasEaten, HasVisited, MadeReview, HasReview
pi_items.source: seed_demodb.py:65-94 creates all vertex and edge classes; demodb schema documented in OrientDB official documentation (https://orientdb.dev/docs/3.2.x/gettingstarted/demodb/index.html)

privileged_info_summary: The task targets OrientDB's built-in demodb database (available since v3.0), which is a real, official OrientDB feature used for travel agency scenarios. The task requires applying six schema constraints to existing classes: Hotels.Name, Hotels.Stars, Profiles.Email, Orders.Price, Restaurants.Name, and Reviews.strictMode. All constraints (MANDATORY, NOTNULL, MIN, MAX, STRICTMODE) are valid OrientDB SQL features. The database uses synthetic data generated at runtime via seed_demodb.py (not external downloads), with realistic hotel names, restaurant names, and country names embedded in the seeding script. The verifier.py correctly checks for all specified constraints by examining the database schema snapshot returned from the OrientDB REST API, comparing property-level attributes and class-level settings against expected values.
